ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Acre, Alagoas, Entire country) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "parent_region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent region (if applicable, e.g., the region is a state, the parent region is the country) */,
  "geo_code" TEXT /* Geographic code for the region (e.g., ISO 3166-2) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the region's centroid */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the region's centroid */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the region's centroid */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_regions_parent_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "epidemiological_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region the data pertains to */,
  "cases_in_past_week" INTEGER /* Number of cases reported in the past week */,
  "reported_prevalence" REAL /* Reported prevalence of the disease in the region */,
  "underreporting_factor" INTEGER /* Factor accounting for underreporting of cases */,
  "delay_factor" REAL /* Factor accounting for delays in reporting */,
  "estimated_prevalence" REAL /* Estimated prevalence of the disease in the region */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the data was recorded */,
  "disease_code" TEXT /* Code for the disease (e.g., COVID-19, Malaria, Influenza) */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group of the affected population (e.g., 0-4, 5-9, 10-14) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the affected population (e.g., Male, Female, Other) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_disease_code FOREIGN KEY ("disease_code") REFERENCES diseases ("disease_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_age_group FOREIGN KEY ("age_group") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_sex FOREIGN KEY ("sex") REFERENCES sexes ("sex")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., State Health Department, National Health Institute) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the data source */,
  "data_provider" TEXT /* Entity responsible for providing the data (e.g., hospital, research institution) */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used for collecting the data (e.g., surveys, electronic health records) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_mappings" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the epidemiological data entry */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "data_collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_mappings_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_mappings_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES epidemiological_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., health official, researcher, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution the user belongs to (e.g., hospital, university) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department within the institution (e.g., epidemiology, public health) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "diseases" (
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease */,
  "disease_code" TEXT /* Code for the disease (e.g., COVID-19, Malaria, Influenza) */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the disease */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disease_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Name of the age group (e.g., 0-4, 5-9, 10-14) */,
  "min_age" INTEGER /* Minimum age for the age group */,
  "max_age" INTEGER /* Maximum age for the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sexes" (
  "sex_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sex */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Name of the sex (e.g., Male, Female, Other) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sex */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sex_id")
)
